query:
INSERT INTO research_docs (lawsuitKey, title, summary, content, createdAt, updatedAt)
VALUES (
  'Illinois Sexual Abuse (Youth Center)',
  'Illinois Sexual Abuse at Youth/Retention Centers — Research Brief',
  'Illinois youth detention and behavioral health facilities where minors were sexually abused by staff or other residents. Approved facilities include Streamwood Behavioral Health, Pavillion Behavioral Health, Lincoln Prairie Behavioral Health, and Riveredge Hospital.',
  '# Illinois Sexual Abuse at Youth/Retention Centers (ILH)

## Overview

This lawsuit covers sexual abuse of minors at Illinois-based behavioral health and youth detention facilities. Victims were patients or residents who were sexually abused by staff members or other residents while in care. The abuse took place at specific approved facilities in Illinois.

## Approved Facilities

- **Streamwood Behavioral Health** (Irving Park, IL)
- **Pavillion Behavioral Health** (Champaign, IL)
- **Lincoln Prairie Behavioral Health** (Springfield, IL)
- **Riveredge Hospital** (Forest Park, IL)

## Who Was Affected

- Minors who were patients or residents at one of the approved facilities
- Must have been under 18 at the time of abuse
- Must be no older than 36 at time of claim (DOB after 1990)
- Must have had an Illinois address at some point

## Nature of Abuse

Sexual abuse by staff members (guards, therapists, night workers) or other residents. Accepted forms include: oral sex, penetration of any kind (digital, vaginal, rectal), fondling under clothing, and cases where staff forced one minor to engage in sex acts with another.

Physical abuse only (fighting, beatings, harassment) is NOT accepted.

## Emotional and Psychological Impact

Victims commonly experience PTSD, depression, anxiety, trust issues, difficulty in relationships, and lasting trauma from abuse that occurred during a vulnerable period of institutionalization. Many were placed in these facilities due to pre-existing mental health challenges, making them especially vulnerable.

## Legal Landscape

Illinois has specific statutes of limitations for childhood sexual abuse claims, with extended windows for victims who were minors at the time. Claims must identify the facility and, where possible, the perpetrator by name or description.

## Key Messaging Angles for Scripts

- Betrayal by institutions meant to provide care and safety
- Vulnerability of minors in state-supervised facilities
- Lasting trauma and psychological harm
- Accountability for facilities that failed to protect residents
- Many victims stayed silent for years — now there is a window to come forward
- Compensation available for pain, suffering, and lasting psychological damage

## Script Notes

- Emphasize the institutional betrayal angle (placed there for help, abused instead)
- Avoid graphic descriptions — focus on emotional aftermath and the right to justice
- Target audience: adults 18–36 who were in Illinois youth/behavioral facilities as minors
- Platforms: Meta, TikTok
- Tone: empathetic, empowering, not exploitative',
  NOW(),
  NOW()
);
